summary: DHH responds to a blogger who dismissed podcasting as pointless just because not everyone will want to listen. He highlights the irony of denouncing podcasting from a weblog, since the same dismissal could apply to blogging itself. The post argues that new media formats like weblogs, podcasts, and eventually video aren't about reaching the largest audience. Their value lies in democratizing creation, not in guaranteeing mass listenership.
key_points: Dismissing podcasting from a weblog is an obvious contradiction, since blogging faced the same criticism
New media formats are never about having the largest audience — they're about enabling individual expression
The pattern of dismissing democratized media will repeat with each new format, including video
money_quotes: I pity Langham for his inability to appreciate the obvious contradiction in denouncing podcasting from a weblog.
Replace podcasting with weblogging and "easier way to download MP3" with "easier way to post to the web".
Weblogs were never about having the largest audience, Podcasting won't be either, and I doubt the video theme following that will be.
key_insight: Every new democratized media format gets dismissed with the same flawed logic — that if not everyone will attract a mass audience, the medium itself has no value.
